content.value: The 'Lower' tones (4, 5, and 6) are spoken in a deeper, more relaxed part of your voice range. Try to drop your jaw slightly and relax your throat.
Tone 4 (Low Falling): The lowest tone. It often sounds like a low, relaxed grumble or a tired sigh.
Tone 5 (Low Rising): Starts low and rises slightly to the middle. It sounds like you are asking a question in a low, gravelly voice.
Tone 6 (Low Level): A low, flat tone. It is similar to Tone 3, but much lower in pitch.
In Cantonese, some sounds are just hummed through the nose without any vowels at all!

pages.title: Lower Tones Mastered!
pages.summary: You've learned to distinguish the deeper tones and hummed syllables.
